rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, demos go first; this is opener-as-primer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 56: 'primer-before-exercise. Names the three moves (merges / soft items / assumptions) that Phase 3 forces. Does NOT name plan-mode approval inflation — that label lands retroactively at exercise P5.' Opener is correctly arming, not naming.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood; this is opener-style for grounded competence.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 10-12 min within budget. Three-things + three-pressures shape is direct-descriptive (not practitioner-attribution-heavy).
Line 19: when-a-plan-is-good-1 registry-expanded prompt with action lead-in line 17. Optional but runnable.
One marker with one-sentence action lead-in.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract — anticipation toward grounded competence.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 57: 'anticipation toward grounded competence. Student leaves the lecture with I know what to look for.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body opens 'Three things a good plan has' — positive discipline. Pressures named second.
Lecture inlines push-back behaviour as 'what you do with this' (line 39); doesn't name Pocock skill. Skill reveal held for M3 per strategy.
Maintainer block line 58 confirms voice check: no honest, delve, landscape-verb, importantly, crucial, ritual, ceremony, practice-as-noun.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — pattern label held for exercise P5.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body never names 'plan-mode approval inflation'; that label lands retrospectively at the exercise.
Body teaches the read mechanic; the felt-experience comes from running push-back-on-the-plan exercise next.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity to strategy M2 lecture role.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block 'Pedagogy: primer-before-exercise' aligns with strategy M2 lecture pedagogy.
